syntax_1227.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_1227.title: On GUI click
syntax_1227.event_values: event-player, event-item, event-integer, event-string, event-inventory, event-clicktype

syntax_1234.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1234.title: Has Custom
syntax_1234.syntax_pattern:
%itemstack% has [a] custom enchantment [%-customenchantment%]
%itemstack% has(n't| not) [a] custom enchantment [%-customenchantment%]

syntax_1235.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1235.title: Has GUI
syntax_1235.syntax_pattern:
%player% has [a] gui
slot %number% of %player% is a gui
%player% does(n't| not) have [a] gui
slot %number% of %player% is(n't| not) [a] gui

syntax_1237.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1237.title: Is Ageable
syntax_1237.syntax_pattern:
%entities% ((is|are) ageable|can grow up)
%entities% ((is|are)(n't| not) ageable|can(n't| not) grow up)

syntax_1244.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1244.title: Command with Permission
syntax_1244.description: Make the player execute a command with a temporary permission, when the player finishes executing the command, the permission is removed.
syntax_1244.syntax_pattern:
[execute] [the] command %strings% by %players% with perm[ission][s] %strings%
[execute] [the] %players% command %strings% with perm[ission][s] %strings%
(let|make) %players% execute [[the] command] %strings% with perm[ission][s] %strings%

en: Make the player execute a command with a temporary permission, when the player finishes executing the command, the permission is removed.

syntax_1249.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1249.title: Gui Properties
syntax_1249.syntax_pattern:
change gui inventory to name %string% and size %number%
change gui shape [of (items|actions)] to %strings%
change gui properties of inventory to name %string% [with %-number% row[s]] and shape [of (items|actions)] to %strings%

syntax_1254.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1254.title: Register Recipe
syntax_1254.syntax_pattern:
(create|register) [new] [custom] shaped recipe with (return|result) %itemstack% using [ingredients] %itemstacks% [with shape %-strings%]
(create|register) [new] [custom] shapeless recipe with (return|result) %itemstack% using [ingredients] %itemstacks%
(create|register) [new] [custom] furnace recipe with (return|result) %itemstack% using [source] %itemstack% [[and] with experience %-number%]

syntax_1256.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1256.title: Unformat GUI
syntax_1256.syntax_pattern:
(unformat|remove|clear|reset) [the] gui slot %numbers% of %players%
(unformat|remove|clear|reset) [all] [the] gui slots of %players%

syntax_1243.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1243.title: Run When Closing GUI
syntax_1243.description: It should be used in [creating or editting a gui](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1245), it is just an extra option to run a code before it closes. This is optinal.

en: It should be used in [creating or editting a gui](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1245), it is just an extra option to run a code before it closes. This is optinal.

syntax_1260.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1260.title: All Recipes
syntax_1260.description: Returns all server recipes. You can get the [ingredients](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1285) and the [result item](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1305).

en: Returns all server recipes. You can get the [ingredients](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1285) and the [result item](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1305).

syntax_1261.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1261.title: All commands
syntax_1261.description: Returns a list containing all registered commands or scripts commands only.

en: Returns a list containing all registered commands or scripts commands only.

syntax_1262.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1262.title: Alphabetical order
syntax_1262.description: Will return a list of [objects](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2158) organized in alphabetic order.

en: Will return a list of [objects](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2158) organized in alphabetic order.

syntax_1263.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1263.title: Command info
syntax_1263.syntax_pattern:
[the] description of command %string%
command %string%'[s] description
[the] main [command] of command %string%
command %string%'[s] main [command]
[the] permission of command %string%
command %string%'[s] permission
[the] permission message of command %string%
command %string%'[s] permission message
[the] plugin [owner] of command %string%
command %string%'[s] plugin [owner]
[the] usage of command %string%
command %string%'[s] usage
[the] aliases of command %string%
command %string%'[s] aliases
[the] file [location] of command %string%
command %string%'[s] file location

en: Get informations about a command.

syntax_1273.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1273.title: Evaluate Function
syntax_1273.syntax_pattern:
result of function %string% [with <.+?>]
result of function %string(<.+?>)

syntax_1277.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1277.title: GUI Value
syntax_1277.syntax_pattern:
gui-slot
gui-raw-slot
gui-hotbar-slot
gui-inventory
gui-inventory-action
gui-click-(type|action)
gui-cursor[-item]
gui-[(clicked|current)-]item
gui-slot-type
gui-player
gui-players
gui-inventory-name
gui-slot-id
gui

syntax_1291.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1291.title: Last Damage Cause
syntax_1291.syntax_pattern:
[the] last damage cause of %livingentity%
%livingentity%'[s] last damage cause

syntax_1308.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1308.title: Knowledge Book
syntax_1308.description: It returns a knowledge book (1.12+ only) with given recipes.

en: It returns a knowledge book (1.12+ only) with given recipes.

syntax_1309.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1309.title: Knowledge Recipes
syntax_1309.description: It returns a list of recipes of a knowledge book. You can either set, add and remove recipes, and clear/delete them all.

en: It returns a list of recipes of a knowledge book. You can either set, add and remove recipes, and clear/delete them all.

syntax_1310.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1310.title: Last GUI/GUI from id
syntax_1310.description: It is used to return the last created gui or a gui from a string id.

en: It is used to return the last created gui or a gui from a string id.

syntax_1312.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1312.title: offline Player from UUID
syntax_1312.description:
It get a player from a UUID. If you have Bensku's fork dev23+, you can just use [parse expression](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:901) using the uuid.
Also, the uuid needs to be from a player that already played on your server, else it will return null value.

en:
It get a player from a UUID. If you have Bensku's fork dev23+, you can just use [parse expression](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:901) using the uuid.
Also, the uuid needs to be from a player that already played on your server, else it will return null value.

syntax_1313.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1313.title: Online Time
syntax_1313.description: Returns a [timespan](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2167) of online time of [player](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2160) or of the server. Only the online time of player can be edited.

en: Returns a [timespan](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2167) of online time of [player](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2160) or of the server. Only the online time of player can be edited.

syntax_1314.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1314.title: Player Data
syntax_1314.description: Returns a [offline player](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2159) as it was a player, which means that you can get some values like money, inventory, enderchest,etc. Maybe all values doesn't work. If you want to change these values, you will have to [save player data](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1255).

en: Returns a [offline player](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2159) as it was a player, which means that you can get some values like money, inventory, enderchest,etc. Maybe all values doesn't work. If you want to change these values, you will have to [save player data](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1255).

syntax_1315.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1315.title: RGB Color
syntax_1315.description:
Returns the rgb color of [item stack](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2152) or [color](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2138). You can set these values only for item stacks, in this case, leather armors.
The RGB color returns a list with 3 numbers and the other expressions returns which one separated.
syntax_1315.compatible_addon_version: 1.5.3 (single value of items), 1.6 (list values of items and color)

en:
Returns the rgb color of [item stack](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2152) or [color](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2138). You can set these values only for item stacks, in this case, leather armors.
The RGB color returns a list with 3 numbers and the other expressions returns which one separated.

syntax_1316.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1316.title: Rabbit Type
syntax_1316.description: Returns the type of rabbit. e.g. `black`, `black and white`, `brown`, `gold`, `salt and pepper`, `the killer bunny` and `white`.

en: Returns the type of rabbit. e.g. `black`, `black and white`, `brown`, `gold`, `salt and pepper`, `the killer bunny` and `white`.

syntax_1317.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1317.title: Recipe ID
syntax_1317.description: Starting from minecraft 1.12, the recipes now has a unique id. Recipes registered by minecraft will have a id `minecraft:%the result item's name%`, an old recipe plugin will be `bukkit:%random uuid%`, while TuSKe will have its id as `tuske:%last recipe id + 1%`. Other plugins may have a different format, but always following `<plugin>:<key>`.**Only for Shaped and Shapeless recipes**

en: Starting from minecraft 1.12, the recipes now has a unique id. Recipes registered by minecraft will have a id `minecraft:%the result item's name%`, an old recipe plugin will be `bukkit:%random uuid%`, while TuSKe will have its id as `tuske:%last recipe id + 1%`. Other plugins may have a different format, but always following `<plugin>:<key>`.**Only for Shaped and Shapeless recipes**

syntax_1318.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1318.title: Recipe Owner
syntax_1318.description: Starting from minecraft 1.12, recipes have now the register plugin. If it is a minecraft recipe, it will return as `Minecraft`, if it is an old recipe plugin, it will return as `Bukkit` and everything else will return the plugin's name. **Only for Shaped and Shapeless recipes**

en: Starting from minecraft 1.12, recipes have now the register plugin. If it is a minecraft recipe, it will return as `Minecraft`, if it is an old recipe plugin, it will return as `Bukkit` and everything else will return the plugin's name. **Only for Shaped and Shapeless recipes**

syntax_1319.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1319.title: Regex Error
syntax_1319.description: It contains the last error of a regex. It is setted when there is a error while parsing a string as regex, [replacing](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1303)/[spliting](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1304) a string or checking in the [condition](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1241)

en: It contains the last error of a regex. It is setted when there is a error while parsing a string as regex, [replacing](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1303)/[spliting](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1304) a string or checking in the [condition](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1241)

syntax_1320.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1320.title: Regex Pattern
syntax_1320.description: This expression returns some regex patterns that Skript or Bukkit uses most.

en: This expression returns some regex patterns that Skript or Bukkit uses most.

syntax_1321.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1321.title: Split Characters
syntax_1321.description: Split a text with [number](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2157) amount of characters. It is used to split the message in chat (the default is 60) and to item's lore.

en: Split a text with [number](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2157) amount of characters. It is used to split the message in chat (the default is 60) and to item's lore.

syntax_1322.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1322.title: Custom Enchantment
syntax_1322.description: It represents a custom enchantment. The values depends of the id name of [registered enchantment](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1252).

en: It represents a custom enchantment. The values depends of the id name of [registered enchantment](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1252).

syntax_1323.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1323.title: GUI
syntax_1323.description: It represents a gui inventory, where the player can take items away from the inventory. It can be created only with [advanced gui effect](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1259).

en: It represents a gui inventory, where the player can take items away from the inventory. It can be created only with [advanced gui effect](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:1259).

syntax_1324.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1324.title: Recipe
syntax_1324.description: A recipe contains the the ingredients list and the result item.

en: A recipe contains the the ingredients list and the result item.

syntax_1325.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1325.title: Regex
syntax_1325.description: Represents a regex object. For now, it won't have any usage but only for test the pattern. It will have more usage in future versions.

en: Represents a regex object. For now, it won't have any usage but only for test the pattern. It will have more usage in future versions.

syntax_1326.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1326.title: Slot Type
syntax_1326.description: It represents a type of a inventory slot
syntax_1326.type_usage: result slot, crafting slot, armor slot, container slot, quickbar slot, outside slot, fuel slot

en: It represents a type of a inventory slot

syntax_1259.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_1259.title: Make GUI
syntax_1259.description:
Used to format a GUI slot inside of GUI creation/editing section.
See also: https://github.com/Tuke-Nuke/TuSKe/wiki/GUI-Manager (Advanced GUI)
syntax_1259.syntax_pattern:
(make|format) next gui [slot] (with|to) %itemstack%
(make|format) gui [slot] %strings/numbers% (with|to) %itemstack%
(un(make|format)|remove) next gui [slot]
(un(make|format)|remove) gui [slot] %strings/numbers%
(un(make|format)|remove) all gui [slot]

en:
Used to format a GUI slot inside of GUI creation/editing section.
See also: https://github.com/Tuke-Nuke/TuSKe/wiki/GUI-Manager (Advanced GUI)

syntax_2525.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_2525.title: Leader
syntax_2525.syntax_pattern:
%player% is leader of his clan
%player% is(n't| not) leader of his clan

syntax_1242.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1242.title: Cancel Drops
syntax_1242.description: Cancels the drops of items, experiences or both in death events, where it won't drop the **player** items (like gamerule KeepInventory), or cancel the item drop of break block event (for minecraft 1.12+ only).
syntax_1242.syntax_pattern:
cancel [the] drops
cancel [the] drops of [e]xp[perience][s]
cancel [the] drops of (inventory|items)

en: Cancels the drops of items, experiences or both in death events, where it won't drop the **player** items (like gamerule KeepInventory), or cancel the item drop of break block event (for minecraft 1.12+ only).

syntax_2530.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_2530.title: invite Player To Clan
syntax_2530.syntax_pattern:
[make] %player% [a] invite %player% to his clan
send invite of clan from %player% to %player%

syntax_2533.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_2533.title: Remove From Clan
syntax_2533.syntax_pattern:
(remove|kick) %player% from his clan
[make] %player% resign from his clan

syntax_2534.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_2534.title: Send Marry
syntax_2534.syntax_pattern:
[make] %player% invite %player% to marry
send invite of marry from %player% to %player%

syntax_1258.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1258.title: Evaluate
syntax_1258.description:
This effect will run any Skript effect/condition from a given string or piece of code. The difference between SkQuery's evaluate effect and this is basically it returns all syntaxes errors instead of send them to the console, It can evaluate a long amount of code and see them easily instead of beeing in one line. 
For example, you can run a effect from a string, from a piece of code (without beeing quoted) or the entiry section of code.
Starting from 1.8, you can disallow some syntaxes when evaluating. Read more about it at config file (`TuSKe/config.yml`).
syntax_1258.syntax_pattern: eval[uate][ logging [[the] error[s]] in %-objects%][ with safety]: (%-strings%|<.+?>)

en:
This effect will run any Skript effect/condition from a given string or piece of code. The difference between SkQuery's evaluate effect and this is basically it returns all syntaxes errors instead of send them to the console, It can evaluate a long amount of code and see them easily instead of beeing in one line. 
For example, you can run a effect from a string, from a piece of code (without beeing quoted) or the entiry section of code.
Starting from 1.8, you can disallow some syntaxes when evaluating. Read more about it at config file (`TuSKe/config.yml`).

syntax_1307.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1307.title: Virtual inv
syntax_1307.syntax_pattern:
virtual <.+?> [inventory] [with size %-number%] [(named|with (name|title)) %-string%]
virtual <.+?> [inventory] [with %-number% row[s]] [(named|with (name|title)) %-string%]
virtual <.+?> [inventory] [(named|with (name|title)) %-string%] with size %-number%
virtual <.+?> [inventory] [(named|with (name|title)) %-string%] with %-number% row[s]

en: Returns the minecraft version of [player](https://skripthub.net/docs/?search=id:2160).

syntax_1248.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_1248.title: Format GUI
syntax_1248.syntax_pattern:
(format|create|make) [a] gui slot [%numbers%] of %players% with %itemstack% [to [do] nothing]
(format|create|make) [a] gui slot [%numbers%] of %players% with %itemstack% to close [(using|with) %clicktype% [(button|click|action)]]
(format|create|make) [a] gui slot [%numbers%] of %players% with %itemstack% to (run|exe[cute]) [(using|with) %clicktype% [(button|click|action)]]
(format|create|make) [a] gui slot [%numbers%] of %players% with %itemstack% to [close then] (run|exe[cute]) %commandsender% command %string% [(using|with) perm[ission] %string%][[(,| and)] (using|with) %clicktype% [(button|click|action)]][[(,| and)] (using|with) cursor [item] %itemstack%]
(format|create|make) [a] gui slot [%numbers%] of %players% with %itemstack% to [close then] (run|exe[cute]) function functionName([parameters])[[(,| and)] (using|with) %clicktype% [(button|click|action)]][[(,| and)] (using|with) cursor [item] %itemstack%]
(format|create|make) [a] gui slot [%numbers%] of %players% with %itemstack% to (run|exe[cute]) [gui [click]] event

en: See also: https://github.com/Tuke-Nuke/TuSKe/wiki/GUI-Manager (Simple GUI)

syntax_1311.creator: Tuke_Nuke
syntax_1311.title: Max Durability
syntax_1311.description: Returns the max durability of [item stack](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=2152). i.e. 1561 for diamond sword. You can cancel it.

en: Returns the max durability of [item stack](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=2152). i.e. 1561 for diamond sword. You can cancel it.

syntax_1245.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_1245.title: Create a GUI
syntax_1245.description:
It creates a new gui with a given id (optional), using a base inventory, and a shape (optional)
For more info, read [this](https://github.com/Tuke-Nuke/TuSKe/wiki/GUI-Manager)

en:
It creates a new gui with a given id (optional), using a base inventory, and a shape (optional)
For more info, read [this](https://github.com/Tuke-Nuke/TuSKe/wiki/GUI-Manager)
